headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 launch dominates the window, with users contesting its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8.
body: Anthropic's Fable 5 drew the bulk of new coverage, spanning live builds, benchmark reviews against GPT 5.5, and a user report that the model consumed 45% of a weekly quota in a single day, against the model's increased capability and steep pricing. Separately Anthropic reversed a policy that would have silently degraded Claude on frontier LLM development tasks, conceding it mistraded transparency for safeguards. On the model side NVIDIA shipped Nemotron 3 in Nano, Super, and Ultra sizes combining hybrid Mamba-Transformer layers with latent mixture of experts and multitask token prediction, and Groq's Jonathan Ross detailed LPU inference economics through Jevons Paradox. Post-training entered as a new concept while RLHF, LLMs, and several decoding and ablation techniques broke out, and IBM highlighted AI-driven zero-day vulnerability detection.

cited_items.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
